events.title: Stone Age Prison Break
events.name: Chemical Engineering Society

overview.description: Stone Age Prison Break is an escape-room where teams solve science, chemistry, and logic puzzles to free Chrome from a stone prison.

rules: Teams solve science, chemistry, and logic puzzles to free Chrome from a stone prison.
The escape concludes with NaOH as the final 'key.'

events.title: DEA Audit
events.name: Chemical Engineering Society

overview.description: DEA Audit is an investigative simulation where participants analyze logs and evidence after a chemical plant explosion to uncover whether the disaster resulted from negligence or sabotage.

rules: Teams investigate logs and evidence using Root Cause Analysis.
Final reports determine negligence or sabotage, scored on accuracy and adaptability.

events.title: The Scoop Stop
events.name: Chemical Engineering Society

overview.description: An interactive ice cream station where participants make ice cream using the bag-in-bag method, demonstrating freezing point depression, heat transfer, and phase transitions in a fun, hands-on way.

rules: Participants make ice cream using the bag-in-bag method.
The activity demonstrates freezing point depression, heat transfer, and phase transitions.

events.title: #1 No Bugs Given
events.name: Computer Science and Engineering Society

overview.description: No Bugs Given is a competitive 1v1 debugging tournament where players race to identify and fix flaws in programs faster than their opponent.

rules: Participants face knockout rounds and must debug programs for syntax and logic errors
Strict time limit, with no AI tools permitted

events.title: CSES Arcade
events.name: Computer Science and Engineering Society

overview.description: An intense 3D, two-player duel built on suspense, strategy, and quick thinking, enhanced by immersive visuals and sound design.

rules: Players take alternating turns with randomized live or dud shells.
The round continues until only one player remains.

events.title: Math Relay Race
events.name: Mathematical Society

overview.description: The Math Relay Race is a three round challenge that blends speed, logic, and problem-solving. Presents deeper, conceptual math problems that demand clarity and reasoning.

rules: Only one final submission per team allowed.
In case of a tie, fastest total completion time wins.

events.title: Treco Hunt
events.name: Mathematical Society

overview.description: Gear up for Treco Hunt – a thrilling race of riddles, quizzes, and hidden clues across 5 exciting rounds! Solve, unlock, and decode your way to the final treasure before anyone else!

rules: Teams must complete each round in order to move to the next.
The first team to reach the venue with the final code wins.

events.title: bugbusters pyFix challenge
events.name: Mathematical Society

overview.description: A 1-hour coding faceoff to spot and fix errors. Solve 4 problems and climb to the top with precision and speed.

rules: Set contains 4 problems, fix buggy code.
-0.5 for incorrect submission.
Time limit: 1 hr

events.title: AI Murder Mystery
events.name: ECE Society

overview.description: Solve a thrilling mystery using AI-bot interrogations and digital clues, connecting evidence logically to uncover the hidden storyline.

rules: Solve the mystery by analyzing clues from AI bot interrogations.
Use additional software-generated hints to uncover the case plot.

events.title: Lie Detector
events.name: ECE Society

overview.description: Answer thought-provoking questions while connected to a lie detector, revealing surprising truths and playful lies in a safe environment.

rules: Take turns honestly and respect others' privacy.
Enjoy the interactive experience of uncovering truth and deception.

events.title: ChipCraft
events.name: ECE Society

overview.description: Build creative structures, automated systems, and logical circuits in Minecraft, showcasing problem-solving and technical imagination within the time limit.

rules: Collaborate effectively and follow all in-game rules.
Showcase creativity, efficiency, and precision in your designs.

events.title: MetaRush
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Meta-Rush” is an adventurous race across multiple routes with exciting checkpoints, where teams unlock clues via QR codes, complete challenges, and compete to finish first with the highest score. It’s a perfect mix of strategy, speed, and fun!

rules: Teams race through 6–8 checkpoints, unlocking clues via QR codes.
Points (+4 correct, -2 wrong, 0 dares) and time decide the winner.

events.title: MetaDart
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Meta-Dart” is a fun blend of skill and knowledge where each dart throw decides your challenge. The closer you land to the center, the easier the question; miss wide, and tougher material-based questions await!

rules: Players throw a dart at a 12-section material board.
Closer to center = easier questions; farther = tougher questions.

events.title: Metalusion
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Metalusion” brings futuristic materials like self-healing compounds and smart alloys to life, showcasing their fascinating real-world applications. Visitors can explore how these innovations reshape technology.

rules: Present special materials, like self-healing materials, at the stall.
Explain their interesting applications to the audience.

events.title: Gesture Drift
events.name: Electrical Engineering Society

overview.description: The Event is a thrilling two-player competitive race where participants control their cars exclusively through hand gestures.

rules: Two players race gesture-controlled cars on a hurdle-filled track.
The first to cross the finish line wins, using only hand gestures—no remotes allowed.

events.title: Dance O'Watt
events.name: Electrical Engineering Society

overview.description: A fun dance game on energy tiles where every step follows the on-screen moves and generates electricity. Play, compete, and power up while you dance!

rules: This team-based interactive dance event challenges players to follow on-screen steps in sync on a 3×3 energy-harvesting tile grid.
Each correct move boosts their score, but one mistake ends the game and locks in their final score.

events.title: Opti-Mystery
events.name: Electrical Engineering Society

overview.description: In this event, one must synchronize strobe lights with rotating objects to reveal and decode hidden patterns for points.

rules: Participants must adjust the strobe frequency carefully, synchronize it with the rotating object, and decode the hidden information.
The patterns may reveal numbers, letters, or even riddles. The faster you decode, the higher you score!

events.title: Track to Trivia: F1 Quiz
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: The F1 Quiz includes online qualifiers, a Grand Prix round (riddles, rapid-fire, strategy, buzzer), and a Final Lap (individual round).

rules: In buzzer rounds, the first team to press gets to answer; wrong answers incur penalties.
No audience discussion or use of phones/internet is allowed.

events.title: Mechanical Kit Assembly
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: In Mechanical Kit Assembly, participating team will be given a dismantled kit and they will have 45 minutes to assemble it.

rules: Points will be awarded to team based on time taken to assemble the kit and percentage of kit assembled accurately.
The team with maximum points will be declared winner.

events.title: OFF TRACK: RC Car Showdown
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: Fun races (3 laps each) at B-312, Mech Dept, 10 AM-12 PM & 2 PM-5 PM. Overtaking/pushing allowed, first to finish wins, no prizes.

rules: Each race has 3 laps on a circuit with turns, intersections, and elevation changes; overtaking and pushing are allowed.
Participants complete all laps for fun; the first to finish is the winner, with no prizes awarded.

events.title: Neon Cricket
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: Each team has 6 players; matches are 6 overs per innings with 1 over per bowler, runs awarded by walls hit, and penalties for no-balls/wides.

rules: Teams of 6 play 6-over innings, scoring via wall hits and bounces.
Outs include bowled, catch, run-out, stumped, LBW, hit-wicket, and one-handed catches.

events.title: Microsope Hunt
events.name: Biotechnology Society

overview.description: Join the Microscope Treasure Hunt — a fun, hands-on challenge where teams explore microscope slides like onion peel, pollen grains, sugar crystals, and hair to uncover hidden clues visible only under the lens. Each clue (a letter, number, or symbol) leads you closer to the final code and the treasure! Earn bonus points for identifying samples or solving riddles. The fastest team wins exciting goodies, certificates, and the title of “Microscope Master.”Join the Microscope Treasure Hunt — a fun, hands-on challenge where teams explore microscope slides like onion peel, pollen grains, sugar crystals, and hair to uncover hidden clues visible only under the lens. Each clue (a letter, number, or symbol) leads you closer to the final code and the treasure! Earn bonus points for identifying samples or solving riddles. The fastest team wins exciting goodies, certificates, and the title of “Microscope Master.”

rules: ⁠Handle microscopes carefully — do not adjust lenses unnecessarily.
Teams must move to the next station only when instructed or after finding the clue.

events.title: Poster Presentation
events.name: Biotechnology Society

overview.description: Visit our Research Poster Showcase to explore fascinating posters on research studies, projects, and case studies. Participants can simply share a soft copy of their posters, and we’ll take care of printing and displaying them at the venue. A perfect platform to share ideas, inspire others, and celebrate research creativity!

rules: Posters must be original, biotechnology-related, and properly referenced if external sources are used.
Entries can be individual or in teams of two, and the poster size must not exceed A2 (42 cm × 59.4 cm).

events.title: Immunofight
events.name: Biotechnology Society

overview.description: Immunofight is an action-packed, team-based challenge that brings biology to life! In this thrilling tournament, the Pathogen team attacks while the Immune System team defends—using colored dodgeballs to represent their roles. Teams of 6–8 will combine strategy, speed, and teamwork in an intense face-off to emerge as the ultimate Biotech Champions of Technozion!

rules: Two Teams, Two Roles: Choose your side – the relentless Immune System or the crafty Pathogen.

Immune System's Goal: Eliminate all Pathogens by hitting them with foam 'antibody' balls. Pathogen's Goal: Survive by dodging! Catch a ball to eliminate the thrower. Answer biotech trivia questions to revive your fallen teammates.

events.title: Dusk till Dawn
events.name: Biotechnology Society

overview.description: Step into the shoes of a forensic investigator in this thrilling, chemistry-driven challenge! Teams will race against time to uncover hidden evidence using luminol spray, revealing glowing clues that vanish in under a minute. Sharpen your observation, teamwork, and problem-solving skills as you piece together the mystery and identify the culprit before the truth fades away. A perfect fusion of science, suspense, and strategy!

rules: Teams have limited time to observe glowing clues revealed by luminol and must quickly memorize or sketch them before they fade.
After the glow disappears, teams collaborate to analyze clues and identify the culprit within the allotted time.

events.title: Kon Banega Civil Engineer
events.name: Civil Engineering Association

overview.description: 'Kon Banega Civil Engineer' is a fast-paced quiz competition testing participants' knowledge in civil engineering. Teams compete through multiple rounds, leading to an exciting 'Hot Seat' finale where the top scorers battle for victory.

rules: Teams compete in multiple quiz rounds to advance.
Top three teams enter the 'Hot Seat' round to determine the final winners.

events.title: Earthquake Resistant Building
events.name: Civil Engineering Association

overview.description: 'Earthquake Resistant Building Model' challenges teams to design and construct a 4-storey structure using limited materials that can withstand rigorous load and shake tests. It's a test of creativity, engineering skill, and structural strength!

rules: Design and build the strongest model possible.
Models must withstand rigorous load and shake table tests

events.title: Model mania
events.name: Physics Society

overview.description: Participants have to present their model and will be judged on the basis of their model and presentation.

rules: Participants have to present their model and will be judged on the basis of their model and presentation.
The model must be original and must be based on the theme of the event.

events.title: Starlight Sparkathon
events.name: Physics Society

overview.description: Sparkathon is a platform for young innovators to showcase their creativity and research in physics. Participants submit their project ideas via Google Form, after which the top 10 are shortlisted to present their models. From these, the 3 most innovative and impactful projects will be crowned winners. It’s an exciting opportunity to explore practical physics applications and spark new ideas!

rules: Participants must submit original project ideas related to physics through the provided Google Form.
Shortlisted finalists must present their project models within the given time limit for evaluation.
